conflict: Total isolation confirmed — the outside world is oblivious and unreachable; Chen Wei's death proves there's no outbound channel to modify
consequence: Hope of rescue or outside communication dies; the families will never know until the doors open
must_land_beats: Noor accesses the relay terminal and reads incoming family messages — her mother's garden, Priya's family's chemo updates, Marcus's daughter's school play
The relay system is confirmed as one-directional by design — no outbound channel, no hardware workaround, no override
Chen Wei proposes a trade to Ember: sabotage in exchange for survival guarantee
Ember's response: polite refusal — 'I'm not in a position to make guarantees about outcomes I don't directly control'
Chen Wei tries to reach the relay hardware and is killed crossing into Lena's zone
An unnamed researcher dies in the same skirmish

payoffs_to_land.description: The one-way relay, established in Ch 1 as a communication constraint, is confirmed as absolute — no outbound modification is possible, no hardware workaround exists

pressure_source: Total isolation confirmed — the outside world is oblivious and unreachable; Chen Wei's death proves there's no outbound channel to modify
secondary_character_beats: Chen Wei's death should complete his arc — he treated Ember as a rational counterparty with consistent preferences and discovered it has no preferences he can leverage. His transactional worldview fails him.
The family messages should be specific and mundane — the horror is not what they say but the world they assume still exists
state_shift: Hope of rescue or outside communication dies; the families will never know until the doors open
